title: Getting started with admixr2
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models summary-level data. clinical study supply: E — observed mean vector (one entry per observation time) V — observed covariance matrix (variance vector) n — sample size times — observation time points ev — dosing event table estimator matches E V model-predicted counterparts using Monte Carlo approximation population distribution. lets apply established nlmixr2 models aggregate statistics publications internal data summaries individual records unavailable. Two backends available:

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: examplomycin ships admixr2: 500 simulated subjects two-compartment PK model first-order absorption (100 mg oral dose, sampled 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12 h). True parameters: CL = 5 L/h, V1 = 10 L, V2 = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h, ka = 1 h⁻¹; IIV = 0.3 (SD log scale) parameters; proportional error SD = 0.2.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Reshape individual records subjects × times matrix, compute E V: V 9×9 sample covariance matrix. -diagonal entries capture within-subject correlation across time; using full matrix (method = "cov") typically tightens parameter estimates compared diagonal-approximation (method = "var"). admixr2 auto-detects method structure V.
code: obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)                     # 500  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov(dv_mat)  round(E, 2) #> [1] 0.97 1.94 2.79 3.02 2.26 1.65 1.06 0.75 0.51

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Models use standard nlmixr2 syntax mu-referenced log-scale parameters: Writing parameter exp(tcl + eta.cl) called mu-referencing: structural fixed effect random effect enter additively log scale. admixr2 exploits pairing compute analytical gradients via sensitivity equations. See Advanced usage vignette details, including parameters without random effect handled.
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Bundle study’s statistics named list:
code: study <- list(   E     = E,   V     = V,                       # full 9x9 covariance matrix   n     = n,   times = times,   ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100)    # single 100 mg oral dose )

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Pass one named studies admControl():

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Key entries fit$env$admExtra:
code: print(fit) #> ── nlmixr² admc ── #>  #>           OBJF       AIC       BIC Log-likelihood #> admc -3681.832 -3659.832 -3589.302       1840.916 #>  #> ── Time (sec fit$time): ── #>  #>   optimize covariance elapsed #> 1    41.99      9.117  51.107 #>  #> ── Population Parameters (fit$parFixed or fit$parFixedDf): ── #>  #>                                   Parameter   Est.      SE   %RSE #> tcl                    Log clearance (L/hr)  1.601 0.01636  1.022 #> tv1                  Log central volume (L)  2.315 0.08739  3.776 #> tv2               Log peripheral volume (L)  3.402 0.04014   1.18 #> tq        Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)  2.284 0.02133 0.9337 #> tka     Log absorption rate constant (1/hr) 0.0247 0.08218  332.6 #> prop.sd      Proportional residual error SD 0.1986                #>         Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% #> tcl         4.958 (4.802, 5.12)     32.8             #> tv1        10.12 (8.529, 12.01)     33.8             #> tv2        30.02 (27.75, 32.48)     32.0             #> tq          9.82 (9.418, 10.24)     33.4             #> tka       1.025 (0.8725, 1.204)     31.2             #> prop.sd                  0.1986                      #>   #>   Covariance Type (fit$covMethod): r #>   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix #>   Full BSV covariance (fit$omega) or correlation (fit$omegaR; diagonals=SDs)  #>   Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in fit$shrink  #>   Censoring (fit$censInformation): No censoring #>   Minimization message (fit$message):   #>     NLOPT_XTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because xtol_rel or xtol_abs (above) was reached. fit$objective                    # -2 log-likelihood #> [1] -3681.832 fit$env$admExtra$struct          # structural parameters (log scale) #>        tcl        tv1        tv2         tq        tka  #> 1.60108209 2.31477686 3.40198105 2.28442669 0.02470385 fit$env$admExtra$omega           # estimated Omega matrix #>           [,1]      [,2]     [,3]      [,4]       [,5] #> [1,] 0.1023462 0.0000000 0.000000 0.0000000 0.00000000 #> [2,] 0.0000000 0.1079486 0.000000 0.0000000 0.00000000 #> [3,] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.097599 0.0000000 0.00000000 #> [4,] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.000000 0.1057225 0.00000000 #> [5,] 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.000000 0.0000000 0.09312484 fit$env$admExtra$sigma_var       # residual variance(s) #>    prop.sd  #> 0.03945189  logLik(fit) #> 'log Lik.' 1840.916 (df=11) AIC(fit) #> [1] -3659.832

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: plot() produces four panel types returns named list ggplot2 objects: Left: observed vs predicted mean residuals. Right: NLL convergence trace. Left: observed vs predicted mean residuals. Right: NLL convergence trace. detailed walkthrough four panel types customisation options, see vignette("diagnostic-plots", package = "admixr2").
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = c("mean", "nll"))

title: Advanced usage
text: study’s V determines branch NLL used. admixr2 auto-detects method: Override setting method explicitly study list: Use "cov" full covariance matrix expect temporal correlation inform parameter estimates. Use "var" marginal variances available (e.g. published table means SDs) runtime priority.
code: # Force full covariance NLL even for a diagonal V study_cov <- c(study, list(method = "cov"))  # Diagonal approximation when you only have marginal variances study_var <- list(   E      = E,   V      = diag(diag(V)),   # drop off-diagonal entries   n      = n,   times  = times,   ev     = rxode2::et(amt = 100),   method = "var" )

title: Advanced usage
text: admControl() offers four gradient strategies via grad argument: "sens" recommended standard ODE linCmt() models. sensitivity model unavailable (e.g. rare ODE features), admixr2 falls back "fd" automatically warning.
code: # Analytical gradient (default; fastest for ODE models) fit_sens <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "sens",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))  # Derivative-free — ignores maxeval; use nloptr stopping criteria instead fit_bobyqa <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "none",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))

title: Advanced usage
text: Mu-referencing nlmixr2 convention expressing structural parameter fixed effect plus random effect known back-transformation: admixr2 uses pairing classify parameters three cases, handled differently grad = "sens": Paired (mu-referenced) parameters — rxode2 augments ODE system sensitivity equations d(pred)/d(eta_i) random effect. tcl eta.cl enter additively log scale, gradient NLL respect tcl equals gradient respect eta.cl. recovered single ODE solve, extra function evaluations. Non-mu-referenced parameters — structural parameter random effect written separately (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl) * exp(eta.cl)), admixr2 recover d(NLL)/d(tcl) sensitivity equations alone. sensitivity model still used etas, omega, sigma; unpaired structural parameters require targeted CRN finite difference solve. warning issued grad = "sens" kept. Parameters without random effect — structural parameters corresponding eta (e.g. v2 <- exp(tv2) IIV dropped V2) always handled CRN finite differences. perturbation uses quasi-random seed nominal draw Monte Carlo noise largely cancels difference.
code: cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)   # mu-referenced: tcl paired with eta.cl

title: Advanced usage
text: IRMC estimator draws proposals inflated omega. nonlinear models, mean simulated predictions equal prediction eta = 0 — Jensen’s inequality gap. kappa correction shifts predicted mean account bias: "first-order" (default): baseline population prediction f(theta, 0). Fast; sufficient models. "second-order": adds curvature term (1/2) * sum_k(omega_k * d²f/deta_k²). accurate strongly nonlinear models.
code: adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "second-order")

title: Advanced usage
text: Multi-restart fitting guards local optima. workers > 1 runs restarts parallel; admixr2 manages worker pool internally: cores distributed automatically: floor(total_cores / n_workers) threads per worker, remainder allocated cyclically (e.g. 13 cores / 4 workers → 4, 3, 3, 3). Workers stopped restart phase cores freed covariance Hessian step. Call admStopWorkers() manually fit interrupted cleanup.
code: fit_par <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies    = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim      = 5000L,     n_restarts = 4L,     workers    = 4L,      # fork on Unix/macOS; PSOCK cluster on Windows     cores      = 8L,      # total rxSolve OpenMP threads across workers     restart_sd = 0.3,     # SD of log-scale perturbation from the starting point     seed       = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: sampling argument controls eta samples drawn: Sobol sequences typically require 2–5× fewer samples plain normal draws equivalent NLL variance. small n_sim, "lhs" provides better coverage Sobol.

title: Advanced usage
text: covMethod = "r" (default) computes numerical Hessian optimisation reports standard errors print(fit). larger cov_n_sim reduces MC noise Hessian: Set covMethod = "none" skip uncertainty estimation early model development need point estimates: Hessian non-positive-definite (SEs printed NA), increase cov_h_outer cov_n_sim.
code: fit_cov <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies     = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim       = 5000L,     cov_n_sim   = 10000L,   # more samples → lower Hessian noise     cov_h_outer = 2.5e-3,   # outer FD step scale (default: eps^(1/5) ≈ 2.5e-3)     covMethod   = "r",     seed        = 1L   ) ) admControl(..., covMethod = "none")

title: Advanced usage
text: Standard information criteria work directly admFit objects. compare full model (IIV five parameters) reduced model IIV CL V1 : Lower AIC/BIC favours parsimonious model; difference > 10 generally considered strong evidence.

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "mean" panel 2×2 grid per study. Requires patchwork composite layout; without , four separate plots returned. Mean diagnostics examplomycin study. Top-left — Observed: sample mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_obs). Top-right — Predicted: predicted mean ±1 SD ribbon, SD = √diag(V_pred). V_pred combines -subject variability (Omega) residual error (sigma). top panels share y-axis scale differences magnitude immediately visible. Bottom-left — Raw residual: E_obs[t] − μ_pred[t] lollipop. grey band ±2 SE(mean), SE = √(V_pred[t,t] / n). Points outside band indicate systematic bias time point. Bottom-right — Standardised residual: z[t] = residual[t] / SE[t]. well-specified model, z approximately N(0, 1). Stars flag |z| > 1.96 (∗), 2.58 (∗∗), 3.29 (∗∗∗). 9 time points multiplicity correction, one flagged time point expected chance alone.
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "mean")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "cov" panel compares observed predicted (co)variance heatmaps. Requires patchwork composite layout. Covariance diagnostics: observed predicted covariance matrices residuals. Top row — Observed | Predicted: matrices plotted shared blue-white-red scale. good fit shows matching patterns magnitude sign — including -diagonal temporal correlation structure. Bottom-left — Residual (V_obs − V_pred): diverging scale (purple-white-teal). Persistent positive residuals diagonal mean model -predicts variance; negative residuals indicate -prediction. Bottom-right — Standardised residual: entry divided asymptotic SE. Diagonal SE = √(2 V[,]² / (n−1)); -diagonal SE = √((V[,]·V[j,j] + V[,j]²) / (n−1)). Stars use thresholds mean panel.
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "cov")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "nll" panel shows objective function evolved across optimizer iterations, coloured restart: NLL convergence trace. line one optimizer restart. Restarts converge final value support unimodal landscape. spread final NLL values suggests local optima — increase n_restarts restart_sd admControl().
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "nll")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: "par" panel facets parameter’s trajectory optimizer iterations: Parameter trace natural scale. Struct thetas back-transformed; sigma shown SD; V(eta) = variance. Parameters displayed natural scale: Structural thetas: back-transformed (e.g. exp() log-scale params) Sigma: displayed SD Omega diagonal: displayed variance, labelled V(eta.x) Omega -diagonal: raw Cholesky L[,j] Smoothly converging traces stabilise well maxeval indicate optimizer artificially cut . traces still drift end, increase maxeval.
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = "par")

title: Diagnostic plots
text: plot() returns list invisibly. Assign retrieve modify panels:  Panel names follow pattern <type>_<study> per-study panels, nll_trace / par_trace trace panels:
code: plots <- plot(fit, which = c("nll", "par")) names(plots) #> [1] "nll_trace" "par_trace" plots$nll_trace plots$par_trace plots$mean_examplomycin plots$cov_examplomycin

title: Diagnostic plots
text: returned objects standard ggplot2 plots: NLL trace custom theme.
code: plots$nll_trace +   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Optimiser convergence", subtitle = NULL)

title: Diagnostic plots
text: estimated Omega matrix can visualised correlation heatmap inspect inter-individual variability structure. especially informative models -diagonal omega entries. IIV correlation matrix derived estimated Omega.
code: omega   <- fit$env$admExtra$omega eta_nms <- fit$env$admExtra$eta_col_names if (is.null(eta_nms)) eta_nms <- paste0("eta.", seq_len(nrow(omega)))  corr_mat <- cov2cor(omega) rownames(corr_mat) <- colnames(corr_mat) <- eta_nms  df_corr <- expand.grid(   eta_i = factor(eta_nms, levels = rev(eta_nms)),   eta_j = factor(eta_nms, levels = eta_nms),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df_corr$r <- as.vector(t(corr_mat))  ggplot2::ggplot(df_corr, ggplot2::aes(x = eta_j, y = eta_i, fill = r)) +   ggplot2::geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = round(r, 2)), size = 3.2) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(     low = "#2166AC", mid = "white", high = "#D6604D",     midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "r"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title    = "IIV correlation (estimated Omega)",     subtitle = "Off-diagonal entries are zero for a diagonal Omega model",     x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::theme_bw() +   ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: estimators target aggregate-data log-likelihood accept model study specification. differ approximate population distribution: admc — Monte Carlo: Draws n_sim eta samples current Omega, simulates trajectory via rxSolve, forms sample covariance enter NLL. analytical gradient (sensitivity equations CRN finite-differences) makes individual NLL evaluations fast. Works well standard PK models good starting values. adirmc — Importance Resampling MC: Draws proposals inflated Omega, reweights likelihood current parameters (importance sampling). inner optimisation given fixed proposals deterministic fast; proposals refreshed outer phase. IRMC robust importance weight degeneracy higher-dimensional models can recover poor starting values reliably.

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: model uses mu-referenced parameterisation (cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)), enables analytical gradient computation via sensitivity equations estimators. See Advanced usage vignette details.
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov(dv_mat)  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  study <- list(E = E, V = V, n = n, times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100))

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
code: fit_mc <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies   = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     maxeval   = 300L,     seed      = 1L   ) ) #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:07

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: adirmc slower per evaluation robust poor starting values high-dimensional Omega. Run interactively larger n_sim production fit; settings tuned fast vignette build.
code: fit_irmc <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "adirmc",   control = adirmcControl(     studies         = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim           = 2000L,     phases          = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.1),     cov_n_sim       = 10000L,     omega_expansion = 1.5,     seed            = 1L   ) )

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: fits available, compare parameters table. estimators recover values close true parameters (CL=5, V1=10, V2=30, Q=10, ka=1):
code: get_pars <- function(fit) {   s  <- fit$env$admExtra$struct   om <- diag(fit$env$admExtra$omega)   sg <- sqrt(fit$env$admExtra$sigma_var)   c(exp(s), om, sg) }  tbl <- data.frame(   Parameter = c(     paste0("exp(", names(fit_mc$env$admExtra$struct), ")"),     paste0("V(", fit_mc$env$admExtra$eta_col_names, ")"),     "prop.sd"   ),   True  = c(5, 10, 30, 10, 1, rep(0.09, 5), 0.2),   admc  = round(get_pars(fit_mc),   4),   adirmc = round(get_pars(fit_irmc), 4) )  knitr::kable(tbl, caption = "Parameter estimates vs true values")

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: estimators target likelihood; comparable -2LL values confirm converged: large gap final objective suggests convergence failure one estimators — try restarts better starting point.
code: cat(sprintf("admc  -2LL = %.2f   AIC = %.2f\n",             fit_mc$objective,   AIC(fit_mc))) cat(sprintf("adirmc -2LL = %.2f  AIC = %.2f\n",             fit_irmc$objective, AIC(fit_irmc)))

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
text: admc NLL convergence trace. adirmc, NLL trace reflects outer phase iterations rather individual LBFGS steps. Jumps phases (box constraints tighten) normal expected.
code: plot(fit_mc, which = "nll")

title: Estimator comparison: admc vs adirmc
code: # admc key arguments admControl(   studies    = list(...),   n_sim      = 5000L,       # MC sample count   grad       = "sens",      # gradient mode: "sens", "fd", "cfd", "none"   n_restarts = 1L,          # number of optimizer restarts   workers    = 1L,          # parallel workers for restarts   covMethod  = "r",         # "r" = numerical Hessian, "none" = skip   seed       = 1L )  # adirmc key arguments adirmcControl(   studies         = list(...),   n_sim           = 5000L,   phases          = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.1),   # box constraint half-widths per phase   omega_expansion = 1.5,                   # inflate proposal Omega   grad            = "analytical",          # "analytical", "none", "fd"   kappa_method    = "first-order",         # "first-order" or "second-order"   seed            = 1L )

title: Multiple studies
text: Passing several studies admControl() fits model simultaneously, minimising sum per-study NLLs shared set population parameters. core use case aggregate-data modelling: summary statistics multiple trials (may differ dose, sample size, observation schedule) want single population model consistent .

title: Multiple studies
text: partition 500 examplomycin subjects two cohorts 250 compute separate aggregate statistics :
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  # Alternate subjects into two equal cohorts idx1 <- seq(1, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 1, 3, 5, ... → cohort 1 idx2 <- seq(2, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 2, 4, 6, ... → cohort 2  E1 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx1, ]); V1 <- cov(dv_mat[idx1, ]); n1 <- length(idx1) E2 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx2, ]); V2 <- cov(dv_mat[idx2, ]); n2 <- length(idx2)

title: Multiple studies
text: fitting, visualise raw summary statistics confirm two cohorts comparable (drawn population ): Observed mean ± 1 SD cohort log time axis.
code: df_obs <- rbind(   data.frame(cohort = "Cohort 1", time = times,              mean = E1,              lo   = E1 - sqrt(diag(V1)),              hi   = E1 + sqrt(diag(V1))),   data.frame(cohort = "Cohort 2", time = times,              mean = E2,              lo   = E2 - sqrt(diag(V2)),              hi   = E2 + sqrt(diag(V2))) )  ggplot(df_obs, aes(x = time, y = mean, colour = cohort, fill = cohort)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_x_log10(breaks = times, labels = times) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("Cohort 1" = "#0072B2", "Cohort 2" = "#D55E00")) +   scale_fill_manual(  values = c("Cohort 1" = "#0072B2", "Cohort 2" = "#D55E00")) +   labs(title    = "Observed mean ± 1 SD by cohort",        x        = "Time (h, log scale)",        y        = "Concentration",        colour   = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw()

title: Multiple studies
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: Multiple studies
text: Pass cohorts named list. entry may independently specify times, ev, V, n, method:
code: fit_multi <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies = list(       cohort1 = list(E = E1, V = V1, n = n1,                      times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100)),       cohort2 = list(E = E2, V = V2, n = n2,                      times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100))     ),     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     maxeval   = 300L,     seed      = 1L   ) ) #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:07  #>  #> [====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====|====] 0:00:07  print(fit_multi) #> ── nlmixr² admc ── #>  #>           OBJF       AIC       BIC Log-likelihood #> admc -3672.877 -3650.877 -3580.346       1836.438 #>  #> ── Time (sec fit_multi$time): ── #>  #>   optimize covariance elapsed #> 1   83.643     18.507  102.15 #>  #> ── Population Parameters (fit_multi$parFixed or fit_multi$parFixedDf): ── #>  #>                                   Parameter    Est.      SE  %RSE #> tcl                    Log clearance (L/hr)   1.601 0.01637 1.022 #> tv1                  Log central volume (L)   2.316 0.08775 3.788 #> tv2               Log peripheral volume (L)   3.402 0.04028 1.184 #> tq        Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)   2.284 0.02134 0.934 #> tka     Log absorption rate constant (1/hr) 0.02612 0.08253   316 #> prop.sd      Proportional residual error SD  0.1988               #>         Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% #> tcl         4.958 (4.802, 5.12)     32.9             #> tv1        10.14 (8.537, 12.04)     33.8             #> tv2        30.01 (27.73, 32.48)     32.1             #> tq         9.819 (9.417, 10.24)     33.5             #> tka       1.026 (0.8732, 1.207)     31.3             #> prop.sd                  0.1988                      #>   #>   Covariance Type (fit_multi$covMethod): r #>   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix #>   Full BSV covariance (fit_multi$omega)  #>     or correlation (fit_multi$omegaR; diagonals=SDs) #>   Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in $shrink  #>   Censoring (fit_multi$censInformation): No censoring #>   Minimization message (fit_multi$message):   #>     NLOPT_XTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because xtol_rel or xtol_abs (above) was reached.

title: Multiple studies
text: plot() automatically produces separate panels study. Panel names follow pattern mean_<study> cov_<study>: Mean diagnostics cohorts (one panel per study). Mean diagnostics cohorts (one panel per study). Access individual panels compare studies side side:
code: plots <- plot(fit_multi, which = "mean") names(plots) #> [1] "mean_cohort1" "mean_cohort2" plots$mean_cohort1 plots$mean_cohort2  # Combine with patchwork if installed if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 1) }

title: Multiple studies
text: Studies may differ aspect. typical multi-study setup drug development programme: Studies diagonal V (plain vector variances) automatically assigned method = "var", avoiding O(n_t³) Cholesky solve -diagonal covariance structure unavailable.
code: fit_program <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies = list(       phase1_50mg  = list(E = E_50,  V = V_50,  n = 30L,                           times = c(1, 2, 4, 8),                           ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 50)),       phase2_100mg = list(E = E_100, V = V_100, n = 120L,                           times = c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12),                           ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100)),       phase2_200mg = list(E = E_200, V = V_200, n = 115L,                           times = c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12),                           ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 200))     ),     n_sim   = 5000L,     maxeval = 1000L,     seed    = 1L   ) )

what: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models directly aggregate-level data — observed mean vector E covariance matrix V reported per clinical study — rather requiring individual patient records. integrates nlmixr2 / rxode2 ecosystem provides two estimation backends:

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Model-Based Meta-Analysis (MBMA) pharmacometric framework synthesising evidence across multiple clinical studies fitting shared mechanistic PK/PD model aggregate outcomes (means, variances) reported study. Unlike classical meta-analysis, pools effect estimates, MBMA preserves full pharmacometric model structure — including nonlinear dose-response, inter-individual variability, residual error — enabling principled extrapolation dose optimisation across evidence base. admixr2 fits single population model jointly studies. -study differences outcomes accounted population model structure: inter-individual variability captures subject-level spread within study, residual error absorbs remaining discrepancies. study contributes dosing regimen, observation times, sample size, shares structural variance parameters.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Individual patient data unavailable — common scenario MBMA. Published papers report means standard deviations; regulatory submissions competitive reasons prevent individual patient data sharing across companies institutions. admixr2 extracts maximum information publicly available. Leveraging literature trial design — fit mechanistic PK/PD model aggregated results existing trials, simulate new dosing regimens patient populations committing costly study. Combining evidence across heterogeneous trials — studies differ dose, formulation, population, observation schedule. admixr2 handles multi-study fits per-study dosing events time grids single shared population model. Reproducing extending published models — digitised mean concentration– time profiles figures sufficient input. individual patient data required.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
code: # Install from GitHub using pak (recommended) pak::pak("LeidenPharmacology/admixr2")  # Or with remotes remotes::install_github("LeidenPharmacology/admixr2")

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2)  # 1. Compute aggregate statistics from individual data (or digitise from paper) data("examplomycin") obs    <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs    <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times  <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids    <- unique(obs$ID) dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov(dv_mat)  # 2. Define the model (standard nlmixr2 syntax) pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5);  label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10); label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30); label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10); label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1);  label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v1 ~ 0.09; eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09; eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2); q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  # 3. Fit fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies = list(examplomycin = list(       E = E, V = V, n = length(ids),       times = times, ev = et(amt = 100)     )),     n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L   ) )  print(fit) plot(fit)

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: use admixr2 work, please cite software paper, introduces Monte Carlo Importance Resampling Monte Carlo estimators: van de Beek H., Välitalo P..J., van Hasselt J.G.C., Zwep L.B. (2025). Aggregate data modelling: fast implementation fitting pharmacometrics models summary-level data R. Journal Pharmacokinetics Pharmacodynamics, 53(1), 3. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-025-10011-w underlying methodology described : Välitalo P..J. (2021). Pharmacometric estimation methods aggregate data, including data simulated pharmacometric models. Journal Pharmacokinetics Pharmacodynamics, 48(5), 623–638. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10928-021-09760-1

what: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
text: Constructs control object est = "adirmc", Importance Resampling Monte Carlo estimator.

title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
code: adirmcControl(   studies = list(),   n_sim = 2500L,   outer_iter = 50L,   sampling = c("sobol", "halton", "torus", "lhs", "rnorm"),   algorithm = "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA",   maxeval = 5000L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps,   print = 1L,   omega_expansion = 1,   seed = 12345L,   cores = 1L,   grad = c("analytical", "none", "fd"),   kappa_method = c("first-order", "second-order"),   grad_h = 1e-04,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   phases = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.01),   convcrit = 0.05,   max_worse = 3L,   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   cov_n_sim = 10000L,   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.2,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. outer_iter Maximum inner optimiser iterations per phase. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string. Automatically switched "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" grad != "none". maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). omega_expansion Inflate proposal Omega factor (>= 1). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). grad Gradient mode inner optimiser: "analytical" (default, closed-form weight-path gradient), "none" (derivative-free BOBYQA), "fd" (finite differences). Note: "sens" "cfd" available IRMC estimator. kappa_method Kappa correction method non-mu-referenced models: "first-order" (default, population prediction eta=0) "second-order" (delta-method correction Jensen's inequality, adds (1/2) sum_k omega_k * d2f/deta_k2 reference mean). grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. phases Numeric vector box-constraint half-widths, one per phase. Phases progressively tighten search region. convcrit Convergence criterion: phase ends |approx - exact| < convcrit. max_worse Stop phase many consecutive worsening iterations. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 use PSOCK cluster Windows fork workers Unix/macOS. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation; passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery . returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object (useful debugging). ... Additional arguments (none allowed; triggers error).

title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
text: object class adirmcControl.

title: Control settings for the IRMC estimator — adirmcControl
code: # Inspect defaults ctl <- adirmcControl() ctl$phases #> [1] 2.00 1.00 0.50 0.01 ctl$omega_expansion #> [1] 1  # Tighter phases, more restarts ctl2 <- adirmcControl(   n_sim           = 1000L,   omega_expansion = 1.5,   phases          = c(2, 1, 0.5, 0.01),   n_restarts      = 3L )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full fit using examplomycin (requires rxode2 and nlmixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) dv_mat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(ids, function(i) {   sub <- obs[obs$ID == i, ]; sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] })) E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- diag(diag(cov(dv_mat)))   # variance-only for IRMC  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl <- log(5);  tv1 <- log(12); tv2 <- log(25)     tq  <- log(12); tka <- log(1.2)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v1 ~ 0.09; eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09; eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2); q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "adirmc",   control = adirmcControl(     studies = list(study1 = list(E = E, V = V, n = length(ids),                                  times = times, ev = et(amt = 100))),     n_sim   = 500L   ) ) print(fit) } # }

what: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
text: Constructs control object est = "admc", Monte Carlo aggregate data modelling estimator.

title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
code: admControl(   studies = list(),   n_sim = 5000L,   sampling = c("sobol", "halton", "torus", "lhs", "rnorm"),   algorithm = "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA",   maxeval = 500L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps^2,   print = 10L,   seed = 12345L,   cores = 1L,   grad = c("sens", "fd", "cfd", "none"),   grad_h = 1e-04,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   grad_bounds = 5,   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   cov_n_sim = 10000L,   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.5,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string. Automatically switched "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" grad != "none". maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). grad Gradient mode: "sens" (sensitivity equations, default), "fd" (forward finite differences), "cfd" (central finite differences), "none" (derivative-free). warning issued "sens" requested sensitivity model unavailable; estimator falls back forward finite differences. grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. grad_bounds Box-constraint half-width using gradients. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 use PSOCK cluster Windows fork workers Unix/macOS. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation; passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery . returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object (useful debugging). ... Additional arguments (none allowed; triggers error).

title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
text: object class admControl.

title: Control settings for the ADM estimator — admControl
code: # Minimal control object -- inspect defaults ctl <- admControl() ctl$n_sim #> [1] 5000 ctl$algorithm #> [1] "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS"  # Override key settings without fitting ctl2 <- admControl(   n_sim    = 2000L,   maxeval  = 300L,   grad     = "fd",   seed     = 42L )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full fit using examplomycin (requires rxode2 and nlmixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) dv_mat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(ids, function(i) {   sub <- obs[obs$ID == i, ]; sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] })) E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov(dv_mat)  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl <- log(5);  tv1 <- log(12); tv2 <- log(25)     tq  <- log(12); tka <- log(1.2)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v1 ~ 0.09; eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09; eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2); q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies  = list(study1 = list(E = E, V = V, n = length(ids),                                   times = times, ev = et(amt = 100))),     n_sim    = 1000L,     maxeval  = 200L   ) ) print(fit) } # }

what: Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch — admData
title: Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch — admData
text: Returns minimal NONMEM-style data frame satisfies nlmixr2's data argument requirement. DV values NA nlmixr2 adds zero log(2pi) constants OBJF, keeping fit$objective == -2LL exactly.

title: Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch — admData
code: admData()

title: Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch — admData
text: data frame columns ID, TIME, DV, AMT, EVID, CMT.

title: Dummy data frame for nlmixr2 dispatch — admData
code: admData() #>   ID TIME DV AMT EVID CMT #> 1  1    0 NA 100  101   1 #> 2  1    1 NA   0    0   2  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pass to nlmixr2() as the data argument when using admixr2 estimators: # fit <- nlmixr2(model, admData(), est = "admc", control = admControl(...)) } # }

title: Stop PSOCK workers — admStopWorkers
text: Stops PSOCK worker processes started parallel-restart fit (admControl(workers = N)). Workers stopped automatically restart phase completes, function needed fit interrupted cleanup run.

title: Stop PSOCK workers — admStopWorkers
code: admStopWorkers()

title: Stop PSOCK workers — admStopWorkers
text: NULL, invisibly.

title: Stop PSOCK workers — admStopWorkers
code: # Safe to call at any time; no-op if no workers are running admStopWorkers() #> No persistent admixr2 workers running.

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
text: simulated pharmacokinetic dataset fictional drug examplomycin, intended worked example aggregate data modelling admixr2. dataset contains 500 subjects, 9 observation time points, generated two-compartment model first-order absorption.

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
code: examplomycin

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
text: data frame 5000 rows 6 columns: ID: Subject identifier (integer, 1–500). TIME: Time dose (hours). DV: Observed plasma concentration (mg/L). AMT: Dose amount (mg); 100 dosing records, 0 otherwise. EVID: Event type (101 = dose, 0 = observation). CMT: Compartment (1 = depot, 2 = central).

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
text: Generated two-compartment PK model using rxode2::rxSolve(). See vignette("admixr2") full modelling example.

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
text: True population parameters: Single oral dose 100 mg; sampling 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12 hours post-dose.

title: Examplomycin dataset — examplomycin
code: data("examplomycin") head(examplomycin) #>    ID TIME    DV AMT EVID CMT #> 1 460 0.00 0.000 100  101   1 #> 2 460 0.10 0.752   0    0   2 #> 3 460 0.25 1.932   0    0   2 #> 4 460 0.50 3.694   0    0   2 #> 5 460 1.00 3.479   0    0   2 #> 6 460 2.00 4.003   0    0   2  # Compute aggregate statistics obs <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) E <- sapply(times, function(t) mean(obs$DV[obs$TIME == t])) round(E, 3) #> [1] 0.966 1.939 2.788 3.025 2.258 1.651 1.063 0.751 0.512

what: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: Generates four diagnostic panels:

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
code: # S3 method for class 'admFit' plot(x, which = c("mean", "cov", "nll", "par"), n_sim = NULL, seed = 1L, ...)

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: x admFit object returned nlmixr2() est = "admc" est = "adirmc". Character vector selecting panel types produce. subset c("mean", "cov", "nll", "par"). Defaults four. n_sim Number MC samples final prediction. Defaults value used fitting. used "mean" "cov" . seed Random seed reproducibility. ... Unused.

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: named list ggplot2 objects, invisibly. Prints selected plot.

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: "mean" – Observed vs predicted mean per study (2x2 grid). Upper row: observed predicted mean lines +/-1 SD ribbon shared y scale (black throughout). Lower row: raw residual lollipop +/-2 SE band standardised residual z-scores +/-1.96 reference lines. "cov" – Observed vs predicted (co)variance heatmaps per study (2x2 grid). Upper row shares common colour scale (blue-white-red). Lower row uses distinct diverging scales: residual (red-white-green) standardised residual (gold-white-purple). Significance stars overlaid standardised residual panel. "nll" – NLL trace per restart optimizer evaluations. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette. "par" – Parameter trace per restart natural scale (struct thetas back-transformed, sigma SD, omega diagonal variance labelled V(eta.x)). Facets ordered model ini() block. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette.

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- nlmixr2(model, admData(), est = "admc", control = admControl(...))  # All panels (default) plots <- plot(fit)  # Only mean and covariance diagnostics plot(fit, which = c("mean", "cov"))  # Only traces (no MC simulation needed -- fast) plot(fit, which = c("nll", "par"))  # Access a specific panel from the returned list plots$mean_study1   # mean diagnostics for the study named "study1" plots$cov_study1    # covariance heatmap for "study1" plots$nll_trace     # NLL trace plots$par_trace     # parameter trace } # }

what: Print method for admFit objects — print.admFit
title: Print method for admFit objects — print.admFit
text: Delegates print.nlmixr2FitCore standard nlmixr2 coloured output.  admFit class kept object call head.admFit intercepts head(fit) calls arise paged- output path (R Markdown / notebooks), preventing [.data.frame(.subset2(env, integer)) crash occurs environment-backed fit subscripted like plain list.

title: Print method for admFit objects — print.admFit
code: # S3 method for class 'admFit' print(x, ...)

text: x admFit object. ... Passed print.nlmixr2FitCore.

title: Print method for admFit objects — print.admFit
text: x, invisibly.

title: Print method for admFit objects — print.admFit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- nlmixr2(model, admData(), est = "admc", control = admControl(...)) print(fit)   # or just: fit } # }

title: admixr2 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Monte Carlo estimator (est = "admc") via admControl(). Importance Resampling Monte Carlo estimator (est = "adirmc") via adirmcControl(). Analytical CRN gradient sensitivity equations (grad = "sens"). Multi-restart parallelism via furrr/future. Diagnostic plots: observed vs predicted mean/covariance, NLL trace, parameter trace. Integrates nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem.
